evidence.display: 45 year old woman admitted to T/SICU from the OR following a    transsternal T4
evidence.text: 45 year old woman admitted to T/SICU from the OR following a    transsternal T4 vertebrectomy and mass removal.

evidence.display: Pt. with alcoholic    cirrhosis and had 5L blood loss in OR.
evidence.text: Pt. with alcoholic    cirrhosis and had 5L blood loss in OR.

evidence.display: PMH:    Hx DM2, CRI, ETOH abuse, pancreatitis, and cirrhosis, depression who
evidence.text: PMH:    Hx DM2, CRI, ETOH abuse, pancreatitis, and cirrhosis, depression who    was admitted on [**2130-4-27**]  with new acute low back pain w/ bilateral leg    spasms, and was found on CTA to have T4 vertebral body destruction.

evidence.display: Pt    was seen on [**2130-4-22**] at OSH for atypical chest pain with negative c
evidence.text: Pt    was seen on [**2130-4-22**] at OSH for atypical chest pain with negative cardiac    eval.

evidence.display: One day later after discharge, she re-presented to OSH with    mid-low back pain
evidence.text: One day later after discharge, she re-presented to OSH with    mid-low back pain.

evidence.display: Pt was also recently discharged from [**Hospital 1711**]    Hospital ([**Date ra
evidence.text: Pt was also recently discharged from [**Hospital 1711**]    Hospital ([**Date range (2) 6007**]?) for ETOH detoxification.

evidence.display: Attempts to biopsy    area and resect using posterior approach were abandoned as
evidence.text: Attempts to biopsy    area and resect using posterior approach were abandoned as pt. could    not tolerate prone position.

evidence.display: T4 vertebrectomy via transsternal approach    Assessment:    Long OR course with
evidence.text: T4 vertebrectomy via transsternal approach    Assessment:    Long OR course with large blood loss.

evidence.display: Arrived sedated on propofol and    requiring neo for BP maintenance.
evidence.text: Arrived sedated on propofol and    requiring neo for BP maintenance.

evidence.display: Two right sided chest tubes with    large sanginous output to water seal.
evidence.text: Two right sided chest tubes with    large sanginous output to water seal.

evidence.display: Multiple areas of ecchymosis    especially on back and perineal area.
evidence.text: Multiple areas of ecchymosis    especially on back and perineal area.
Last hct 35, INR 1.6.

evidence.display: Sternal/R    thoracotomy incision closed with dermabond, open to air.
evidence.text: Sternal/R    thoracotomy incision closed with dermabond, open to air.
Intubated.

evidence.display: Lumbar drain in place without drainage.
evidence.text: Lumbar drain in place without drainage.
Lactate 2.9.

evidence.display: Action:    Q 1-2 hour neuro checks done, thoracic CT obtain, CXR X 2 done,    fr
evidence.text: Action:    Q 1-2 hour neuro checks done, thoracic CT obtain, CXR X 2 done,    frequent lab draws, 2 units prbc, 2 units ffp, bag of platelets given.

evidence.display: Q 1 hour chest tube output recorded and patency ensured.
evidence.text: Q 1 hour chest tube output recorded and patency ensured.

evidence.display: Lumbar drain    positioned in effort to obtain 15cc CSF output q 1 hour.
evidence.text: Lumbar drain    positioned in effort to obtain 15cc CSF output q 1 hour.

evidence.display: Dr [**First Name (STitle) **]    assessed drain patency at 01:00.
evidence.text: Dr [**First Name (STitle) **]    assessed drain patency at 01:00.

evidence.display: Response:    Awakes to name while on low dose propofol, follows commands but get
evidence.text: Response:    Awakes to name while on low dose propofol, follows commands but gets    agitated and clamps on endotracheal tube.

evidence.display: Follow commands readily with    arms, wiggles l toes and withdraws R leg to pain
evidence.text: Follow commands readily with    arms, wiggles l toes and withdraws R leg to pain.

evidence.display: CT output slowed,    continued to require low dose neo.
evidence.text: CT output slowed,    continued to require low dose neo.

evidence.display: Initially lumbar drain did not    drain, now with 12cc/hr when level at -5 cm be
evidence.text: Initially lumbar drain did not    drain, now with 12cc/hr when level at -5 cm below shoulder.

evidence.display: Plan:    Continue to monitor neuro status, ct output, labs.
evidence.text: Plan:    Continue to monitor neuro status, ct output, labs.

evidence.display: Consider weaning to    extubation when stabilized.
evidence.text: Consider weaning to    extubation when stabilized.

evidence.display: Coagulopathy ..pt with alcohol cirrhosis    Assessment:    Large abdomen with as
evidence.text: Coagulopathy ..pt with alcohol cirrhosis    Assessment:    Large abdomen with ascities.

evidence.display: Hct 22, INR 1.6, platelets down to 80    multiple areas of ecchymosis and oozing
evidence.text: Hct 22, INR 1.6, platelets down to 80    multiple areas of ecchymosis and oozing from central lines.

evidence.display: Large CT    output    Action:    PRBCs X 2 given, 2 units ffp, 1 bag of platelet
evidence.text: Large CT    output    Action:    PRBCs X 2 given, 2 units ffp, 1 bag of platelets.
Frequent lab    monitoring.

evidence.display: Response:    Decrease in CT output, less oozing from central lines, stabilizing
evidence.text: Response:    Decrease in CT output, less oozing from central lines, stabilizing    hemodynamics    Plan:    Continue to monitor closely.

evidence.display: Pain control (acute pain, chronic pain)    Assessment:    Large thoracic/sternal
evidence.text: Pain control (acute pain, chronic pain)    Assessment:    Large thoracic/sternal incision.
Initially on propofol only.

evidence.display: Was    previously on neurontin and Dilaudid for back pain.
evidence.text: Was    previously on neurontin and Dilaudid for back pain.

evidence.display: Action:    Pain assessment q 2-4 hours, fentanyl iv as well as repositioning for
evidence.text: Action:    Pain assessment q 2-4 hours, fentanyl iv as well as repositioning for    pain reduction    Response:    Pt. nods that pain is acceptable.

evidence.display: Because of intubated status, unable    to use pain scale.
evidence.text: Because of intubated status, unable    to use pain scale.
Plan:    Continue frequent assessment.

evidence.display: Judicious use of narcotics as indicated    combined with positioning techniques.
evidence.text: Judicious use of narcotics as indicated    combined with positioning techniques.

evidence.display: Restart adjunctive pain measures    today (lidocaine patch and neurontin).
